Throughout his career, he has developed deep expertise in Kubernetes orchestration and container security, with a passion for building resilient, scalable platforms. As a strong advocate for cloud security best practices, Yin prioritises implementing defense-in-depth strategies across infrastructure, from secure container pipelines to comprehensive access controls and network security. He is committed to advancing the Kubernetes ecosystem and sharing knowledge with the cloud-native community while continuously exploring innovative approaches to secure, efficient platform engineering

person.name: Badal Kumar Prusty
person.bio: A DevOps engineer with love for sane YAML, resilient clusters, and zero-downtime deploys. I navigate my path in the cloud-native universe through Kubernetes, GitOps, and a generous amount of curiosity. From designing observability pipelines to automating infrastructure, I delight in decluttering complexity into serenity. Still learning, still debugging — and still remaining calm.

person.name: Daniel Bronk
person.bio: Hi! I’m Daniel Bronk, a Cloud Engineer and Kubernetes expert living in Utrecht, Netherlands. I love to closely collaborate within a team to create, discuss and complete projects in the best possible way. My main focus is maintaining Kubernetes clusters, automating the process and creating the cloud infrastructure needed to run the applications on these clusters.

person.name: Jeremy Garcia
person.bio: Jeremy is the VP of technical community and open source at Datadog, where he's responsible for developer advocacy, training, documentation, technical writing, multiple engineering teams, and the open source programs office. An ardent but realistic open source advocate with a pragmatic approach, he is the founder of LinuxQuestions.org, which is one of the largest independent open source communities on the web. An early podcaster, he's currently a presenter on Bad Voltage. He's also on the board of the Linux Fund. Before joining Datadog Jeremy ran a consultancy helping organizations implement open source programs, find viable open source business models, and grow technical communities. He's spoken at a variety of conferences, had a column in Linux Magazine, was an ambassador for Opensource.com, and regularly blogged about the intersection of business and open source. Outside of technology he is an avid runner and foodie, and long time Buffalo Bills fan.

person.name: Solihin Febrian Saputra
person.bio: I am a Kubernetes and cloud-native engineer with a strong DevOps background, specializing in designing and operating scalable, secure, and production-ready platforms. I work extensively across the CNCF ecosystem, including Kubernetes, observability, and platform security, with hands-on experience in multi-cluster environments, hosted control planes, and Kubernetes on OpenStack-based infrastructure. I focus on applying DevOps and GitOps principles to automate infrastructure, improve reliability, and maintain consistent, repeatable platform workflows.

person.name: Alexys Perez
person.bio: I am a seasoned telecommunications specialist with decades of experience in 3GPP RAN technologies for Ericsson. My Cloud Native experience comes from working at Telstra Australia and a Cloud Native sovereign data company. As a solution architect with deep technical leadership, I am an expert in Ericsson Cloud RAN, leveraging its cloud‑native architecture to drive scalable, high‑performance network solutions. My career spans end‑to‑end RAN engineering, cloud-native onboarding, networking, and security, with extensive hands-on involvement in software supply, lifecycle management, and operational support. I look forward to working more closely and sharing knowledge with my fellow Kubernetes colleagues throughout the world.

person.name: Emmanuel Mendoza Sosa

He specializes in Cloud, Infrastructure as Code and Kubernetes, with a strong passion for collaborating with development teams to solve and debug complex problems. Emmanuel likes to learn open-source technologies that enhance and contribute to the DevOps ecosystem.

person.name: Patipat Ponyanan

Passionate about creating customer-focused systems, I’ve worked on projects that enhance user experience and support team success.

person.name: Mayank Sharma

I primarily work with Microsoft Azure and Kubernetes. I have executed multiple cloud migrations and infrastructure rollout projects from scratch.

person.name: Ramamurthy Krishnamurthy
person.bio: Ramamurthy is a DevOps and Cloud Specialist with over 15 years of experience, currently leading Cloud & DevSecOps as a Specialist Infrastructure at Publicis Sapient. With a strong background in digital transformation, He provides technical leadership to engineering and development teams, focusing on Kubernetes, cloud, security and DevOps methodolgy. Passionate about building resilient, scalable systems, Ramamurthy actively shares his knowledge through mentorship, public speaking, and technical leadership. He plays a pivotal role in standardizing DevOps and security practices, ensuring efficiency, reliability, and security across global teams. With extensive hands-on experience in Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, and Istio, he specializes in designing and implementing fault-tolerant, scalable solutions across all major cloud platforms. He is dedicated to helping teams package, deploy, and orchestrate microservices at scale, leveraging cutting-edge technologies to enhance reliability, security, and performance

person.name: Raphael Krüger

My work is all about building scalable, secure, and efficient solutions using modern DevOps practices. I care about clean architecture, automation, and making sure deployments are smooth and reliable. I'm always learning - especially when it comes to the evolving Azure and Kubernetes ecosystems.

person.name: Damilola

person.name: El-Ad Blech

Currently, my professional focus is at DevOps and Cloud Engineering, with the goal of becoming a Kubernetes specialist.

person.name: Laurent Thil

I like building , architecture, making things lean, reliable and efficient. I felt in the Kubernetes wagon a few years ago and never looked back since. Today I help managing Kube infrastructures with CNCF principles and help projects jumping in the band waggon.

person.name: Phillip Steinert
person.bio: I’m an open-source and CNCF enthusiast with a strong passion for Cloud Native technologies. My main focus is on Kubernetes, GitOps, AWS and event-driven architectures that bring reliability, security and efficiency to modern systems. My goal is to enable teams to build resilient, secure and future-ready platforms in the cloud.

person.name: Riccardo Bertelli

That curiosity pulled me into the world of DevOps, where I instantly fell in love with Docker, Kubernetes, and more recently, NixOS. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies, automation, and system design, and I’m always exploring how to make systems more resilient and elegant. When I’m not debugging YAML or optimizing CI pipelines, you’ll probably find me explaining to friends why “just one more container” is a perfectly reasonable hobby

person.name: Hamed Mosavat

Building on this academic foundation, I now work as a senior Site Reliability Engineer at Fortinet, specializing in cloud-native technologies, Kubernetes, containerization, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), and infrastructure automation. My work bridges deep research expertise with practical engineering to ensure the reliability and scalability of cloud systems.

person.name: Stanisław Hellwig

person.name: Weiguang Yang

person.name: Belhedi Chaima
person.bio: Hi, I’m Chaima!  I’m deeply passionate about infrastructure, cloud-native technologies, and everything that powers modern platforms. I discovered this passion the day I realized I could spend hours debugging—driven by patience, curiosity, and genuine love for the craft.  When the Kubeastronaut program was announced, I made it a personal mission to be part of it—and I’m proud to say: goal achieved.  I’m especially drawn to Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, cloud automation, security, and the ever-evolving cloud ecosystem. I love building reliable, scalable systems and finding joy in the small details that make big things work.  Currently, I’m a Platform Engineer at InstaDeep, where collaboration, growth, and lifting each other up are at the heart of what we do. I’m incredibly grateful to the mentors and teammates who’ve supported, challenged, and believed in me—your guidance has shaped my journey.  If there’s any way I can help, contribute, or even exchange knowledge—don’t hesitate to reach out. I’m always happy to share, support, and keep learning—together.

person.name: Muppana Sri Vidya Vihari

I continuously enhance my knowledge of modern cloud-native ecosystems and actively participate in community meetups to share insights and stay aligned with industry advancements. Always eager to grow, I am now exploring AI and ML to integrate these emerging capabilities into my future projects.

person.name: Michael Yao

person.name: Panus Janyapanich

Excited to be part of Kubestronaut and grow with the community! 😀

person.name: Dmitry Shurupov

He also shares Kubernetes and cloud native-related news and tools in the Kubernative Telegram channel (https://t.me/kubernative).

I regularly write technical content exploring practical and timely implementation challenges, and enjoy making complex Kubernetes concepts more accessible.

person.name: Mads Høgstedt Danquah

He has always been interested in how technology works and how people interact with it. Mads is a strong advocate for open communities and the power of open source to level the playing field for the next generation.  His current focus is on platform engineering, with an emphasis on building products that enable the rest of the organization.  In his spare time, he volunteers with the Danish Scout Association and helps organize Kubernetes Community Days in Denmark.

Michael played a pivotal role in creating the Cloud Native Glossary initiative and is an active organizer of CNCF Toronto Meetups. In his free time, he enjoys staying active through skiing, exploring Toronto's on his motorcycle, and tinkering with mechanical projects at home.

person.name: Erdi Erkan

person.name: Andrei-Mihai Zaharia

Today, I run my own IT company, offering specialized services in infrastructure, CI/CD (DevOps), and cloud solutions. If you're passionate about starting your own journey in IT or need expert advice, feel free to reach out.

My goal is to advance Kubernetes best practices and help organizations build reliable, secure, and scalable systems.

person.name: Farid Arbabzadah

person.name: Ation Gilliam

Now, I’m committed to using that journey to teach, motivate, and inspire young people. Through my non-profit organization, The Gift INC, I want to show them that they too can build successful careers in IT.

Previously, he served as a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS, where he partnered with customers on cloud-native and AI/ML architectures. He is passionate about using automation and AI to transform infrastructure into resilient, self-service platforms.

person.name: Mingming Tao

Passionate about dissecting complex issues and advancing operational excellence. Always curious, hands‑on, and eager to solve hard problems.

I enjoy tackling complex technical challenges and delivering innovative solutions. Technology and problem-solving are at the heart of what I do.

person.name: David Thong

My goal is helping to connect the Software Development Community in the Southern Hemisphere. Originally from Argentina, my family and I moved to New Zealand more than 10 years ago, where we still live today, in the beautiful city of Wellington.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: First of all, receiving recognition from the CNCF is an honour and a great support to continue working for the local tech community. It is definitely a great opportunity to open new doors and offer more opportunities to our community and I am very much looking forward to working with many other colleagues and friends in this ambassador program. Thanks!

'k8n') is a GNU/Linux SysAdmin who's in love with the terminal.He works with curiosity and passion on GNU/Linux, Free & Open Source software, and Cloud Native technologies such as Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, Argo projects. person.name: Qing Hao

She has been involved in numerous open source activities over the past few years, including being a speaker at KubeCon, a mentor for OSPP and GSoC many times. She has hosted many open source meetups in Xi’an as early as 2015, and she is now one of the organizers of KCD.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am passionate about open source and sharing, and becoming an ambassador would provide me with more opportunities and motivation to do what I love. I’ve witnessed the CNCF project’s remarkable growth in recent years, and now, with the rise of AI, it’s thriving more than ever. As an ambassador, I look forward to helping more people and projects benefit from open source, which is one of the most exciting aspects of this role.
